Home/Resources/Post an Event

Post an event

Use the right calendar first.

Start with the county and town route that matches the event. Then add media, regional calendars, arts routes, and social/event platforms when the audience fits.

Colfax Las Animas Huerfano

Fast route

Do these first.

Create a public event page, prepare one image and a short blurb, then submit to the closest local calendar before asking wider regional channels.

Colfax Las Animas Huerfano Media

Local first

Submit where the event actually belongs.

Pick the county first, then the nearest town, chamber, tourism page, venue, or public calendar. If the event crosses county lines, submit locally first and then add the regional media routes below.

Colfax County

Raton, Angel Fire, Eagle Nest, Cimarron, Springer, Maxwell, and Moreno Valley routes.

Use Raton routes for downtown, radio, and business visibility; Angel Fire/Eagle Nest routes for visitor-facing mountain events; and Cimarron/Springer routes for small-town community signals.

Raton

Use KRTN, Raton MainStreet, Explore Raton, the City calendar, and local arts routes for downtown events, public programs, festivals, and business visibility.

KRTN calendarRaton MainStreet eventsCity calendar

Angel Fire

Use Visit Angel Fire first for visitor-facing events, then the Chamber and Village calendar for community, parks, resort-area, and civic visibility.

List with Visit Angel FireAngel Fire Chamber annual calendarVillage calendar

Eagle Nest

Use Eagle Nest Chamber and Angel Fire/Moreno Valley routes for lake events, fishing tournaments, arts markets, fireworks, and visitor-facing weekend traffic.

Eagle Nest ChamberAngel Fire Resort events

Cimarron / Springer / Maxwell

Use chamber, radio, school, and county contact routes for smaller-community events. These often need a direct message or email instead of a polished online form.

Cimarron Chamber siteSpringer Chamber FacebookColfax area resources

Las Animas County

Trinidad, Aguilar, Cokedale, Branson, Kim, Starkville, Weston, Stonewall, and countywide routes.

Use Visit Trinidad for visitor-facing events, TLAC Chamber for business-community reach, and CSU Extension/Fair routes for agriculture, 4-H, fair, and education audiences.

Trinidad visitor routes

Start with Visit Trinidad when the event is open to the public and useful to visitors, downtown audiences, arts audiences, or regional travelers.

Submit to Visit TrinidadTourism marketing request

Business + chamber route

Use the TLAC Chamber calendar and chamber contact path for business events, member events, networking, ribbon cuttings, and downtown/county announcements.

TLAC ChamberChamber posting help

Fair, 4-H + Extension

Use CSU Extension and fair routes when the audience is agriculture, youth, 4-H, county fair, community education, food systems, or rural services.

CSU Extension eventsCounty fair schedule

Arts, college + venues

Use Trinidad State, CREATE Trinidad, Main Street LIVE, A.R. Mitchell, Carnegie Library, and Mt. Carmel when the event is hosted by or fits that venue or program.

Trinidad State arts calendarMt. Carmel calendarA.R. Mitchell events

Huerfano County

Walsenburg, La Veta, Cuchara, Gardner, and Spanish Peaks routes.

Use Spanish Peaks Country and the Huerfano Chamber for countywide visibility, then add the town, arts, library, or community-bulletin route that fits the actual audience.

Countywide visitor + chamber

Use Spanish Peaks Country for visitor-facing county events and the Huerfano Chamber for chamber/community calendar review.

Submit to Spanish Peaks CountrySubmit to Huerfano ChamberCounty calendar

Walsenburg

Use Downtown Walsenburg, Spanish Peaks Library, the Fox Theatre route, and county calendar routes for downtown events, public programs, theater, markets, and local notices.

Downtown Walsenburg calendarSpanish Peaks Library events

La Veta

Use La Veta Creative District, SPACe, Francisco Fort, FCPA, La Veta Library, and Estelle Center when the event is arts, music, history, workshops, or creative tourism.

La Veta Creative DistrictSPACe Art in the ParkEstelle Center events

Cuchara + Gardner

Use Cuchara Mountain Park, Cuchara-area community routes, Gardner bulletin routes, Bachman’s weekly calendar, and library/bookmobile routes for mountain and rural audiences.

Cuchara Mountain Park calendarBachman weekly calendarGardner bulletin group

Media + email calendars

Add channels that match the audience.

Use these after the event has a clear public link, date, location, short description, image, cost, and organizer contact.

World Journal

Regional newspaper events route for Huerfano, Las Animas, Colfax, and nearby communities.

Email events deskOpen events page

Bachman weekly calendar

Useful for La Veta, Cuchara, Gardner, Walsenburg, and farmers-market style community notices.

Email Bachman calendarOpen calendar

KCRT / KBKZ

Trinidad-based radio contact with regional signal reach. Ask about announcements, interviews, ads, or public-service options.

Email KCRT / KBKZOpen station site

KRCC

Public radio community calendar for Southern Colorado events. Best for nonprofit, arts, community, and public-interest events.

Submit to KRCC

KUNM

Public radio calendar for New Mexico events. Use for Raton, Colfax, or northern New Mexico events with a wider public-radio audience fit.

Submit to KUNM

LocalStash

Create a public Facebook Event first, then contact LocalStash when the event has Southern Colorado or Northern New Mexico regional appeal.

Open LocalStash

Arts + culture

Use arts routes when the event is creative, cultural, or venue-based.

Venue calendars are not general calendars. Use them when the event is hosted, booked, sponsored, or co-produced there.

Southwest Contemporary

Arts and culture calendar for Colorado and New Mexico events.

Submit arts event

Colorado Fun Guide

Entertainment-focused events for Southern Colorado and the Pikes Peak region.

Submit event

LiveTaos / Taos.org

Northern New Mexico arts and entertainment route. Useful for Angel Fire, Eagle Nest, Cimarron, or Raton events with Enchanted Circle audience fit.

Submit to LiveTaos

Main Street LIVE

Use for events hosted or rented through the Trinidad performing arts venue.

Ask about venue rental

FCPA La Veta

Use for theatre, performances, talks, and booked events at Francisco Center for the Performing Arts.

Book the theater

A.R. Mitchell Museum

Use for museum-hosted arts events, openings, lectures, and possible nonprofit/arts hosting.

Open museum events

Wider reach

Use broader channels only when they fit.

A Trinidad, Walsenburg, Raton, Angel Fire, La Veta, or Cuchara event can sometimes draw Pueblo, Colorado Springs, Taos, or statewide tourism audiences. Use these routes selectively.

Colorado.com

State tourism listing route for Colorado visitor-facing events and eligible tourism partners.

Start Colorado.com route

New Mexico Tourism

Use for Colfax County tourism events, visitor-facing programs, and New Mexico travel visibility.

Open NMTD partner routes

Share Pueblo Events

Use when the event could reasonably draw Pueblo-area audiences.

Open Share Pueblo

Colorado Springs calendar

Use for events with Pikes Peak or southern Colorado audience fit.

Open calendar

Bandsintown / Songkick

Use for concerts and touring performers so fans can discover shows by artist, city, and venue.

Bandsintown helpSongkick help

Download the full list

The research file includes 80 calendar, media, venue, tourism, arts, social, and music-platform routes across all three counties plus selective regional spillover channels.

Download CSV
